title: Earthy Tender Polish Beef Stew
description: A hearty and indulgent Polish dish featuring tender beef, earthy flavors, and a hint of oregano, made with halal-friendly ingredients. This stew is perfect for a cold winter's night, served with crusty bread or over mashed potatoes.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the beef cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove from pot and set aside.
Add another tablespoon of oil to the pot, then add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes, cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the browned beef back to the pot, along with the dried oregano, beef broth, tomato paste, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2.5 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Use a whisk to stir the stew and check the seasoning. Adjust as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh parsley or dill, if desired.
